missionTitle: Evidence viewer v0 — render inspectable evidence packets for merge approval (CAM-CORE-07, Phase-1)

issues.title: Evidence-packet schema types in packages/shared
issues.goal: Define the v1 evidence-packet TypeScript types in packages/shared so the GUI renders a real, typed structure rather than untyped JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/shared exports a packet type carrying every S11 field: attempt_id, issue_id, contract_hash, candidate_sha, base_sha, worker_head_sha, commands[], artifacts[], checks[], reviews[], exclusions[], waivers[], retries, failure_class, verdict, created_at.
Each nested item type (commands, artifacts, checks, reviews, exclusions, waivers) includes its own sha, base_sha, and a class field typed as 'advisory' | 'gating'.
artifacts items type includes path, type, sha256, and scrubbed.
tsc passes and packages/gui imports the type without violating the core/shared import fence.

issues.title: Packet rendering component (sections + gating/advisory + exclusion/waiver lists)
issues.goal: Build the React component that renders a packet's contents — all sections plus metadata — with each item's advisory/gating class visible and the exclusion/waiver lists shown.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a packet fixture, the component renders every top-level section: commands, artifacts, checks, reviews, exclusions, waivers, plus metadata (verdict, failure_class, retries, created_at).
Each rendered item shows its class with a visually distinct advisory-vs-gating marker.
exclusions render their item/reason and waivers render their register_ref/reason/actor.
Component renders without error when a list (e.g. exclusions or waivers) is empty.

issues.title: Artifact previews (logs inline, screenshots inline, traces open locally)
issues.goal: Render artifact previews so a reviewer can inspect logs, screenshots, and traces from within the packet view.
acceptanceCriteria: A log-type artifact renders its text content inline.
A screenshot-type artifact renders as an inline image.
A trace-type artifact exposes an 'open locally' action rather than requiring inline rendering.
Each artifact displays its scrubbed status and sha256.

issues.title: Merge approval screen embeds packet and gates approval
issues.goal: Embed the evidence viewer in a merge approval screen so approval happens against the packet, and no merge is approvable without its packet.
acceptanceCriteria: A merge approval screen embeds the evidence viewer showing the packet for the candidate being approved.
The approve control is blocked/disabled when no packet is present for the candidate.
The gating-vs-advisory distinction is visible on the approval screen.

issues.title: Synthetic packet fixtures and Playwright probe
issues.goal: Provide synthetic evidence-packet fixtures (no real packets exist yet) and a Playwright probe that verifies the viewer renders and gates correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixture packets exist covering both advisory and gating items, all three artifact types (log, screenshot, trace), and non-empty exclusions and waivers.
A Playwright probe loads the viewer against a fixture and asserts each packet section renders and both gating and advisory markers appear.
The probe asserts the approve control is not enabled when a candidate has no packet.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Where does the GUI read evidence packets from — a daemon HTTP endpoint (Fastify) serving packet JSON from the SQLite event log, or directly from local fixture files for v0?
clarifyingQuestions.whyItMatters: This sets the data-access architecture shared across the packet component, artifact previews, and the approval screen, and whether a daemon endpoint is part of this mission.
clarifyingQuestions.assumptionIfUnanswered: The GUI reads packets through a daemon HTTP endpoint returning packet JSON; because no real packets exist yet (S5), v0 wires that endpoint to synthetic fixtures, and the components consume typed JSON without assuming direct filesystem/DB access.

clarifyingQuestions.question: What does 'render inline or open locally' mean concretely for each artifact type — specifically, must traces render inline (e.g. embedded Playwright trace viewer) or is launching them locally acceptable for v0?
clarifyingQuestions.whyItMatters: It sets the scope of I3: inline trace rendering is substantially more work than an 'open locally' file action.
clarifyingQuestions.assumptionIfUnanswered: Logs and screenshots render inline; traces are offered via an 'open locally' action (open the artifact file with the OS/existing viewer) rather than inline.

clarifyingQuestions.question: How does the viewer access artifact bytes and treat the 'scrubbed' flag — are artifact files at a local `path` the viewer can read, and does 'scrubbed' only affect a displayed badge or gate what content is shown?
clarifyingQuestions.whyItMatters: It determines how I3 fetches artifact content and whether scrubbing is a display concern or a content-filtering obligation.
clarifyingQuestions.assumptionIfUnanswered: Artifacts are addressable via their `path` (local filesystem or served by the daemon), the viewer previews the referenced content as-is, and `scrubbed` is surfaced as a badge without additional filtering by the viewer.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Does the v0 viewer render the mission rollup (mission_id, requirement_map, gate_record, per_issue_delivered), or only per-attempt packets?
clarifyingQuestions.whyItMatters: Rendering the mission-level rollup adds a distinct UI surface and typing beyond the per-attempt packet the approval screen embeds.
clarifyingQuestions.assumptionIfUnanswered: v0 renders per-attempt packets only; rendering the mission rollup is deferred to a later mission (registry/rollup surface), and no rollup UI is built here.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Does this mission build the merge approval screen itself (the GUI is an empty scaffold), or only the embeddable viewer that a separately-owned approval screen will host?
clarifyingQuestions.whyItMatters: It sets whether I4 delivers a minimal host screen that demonstrably gates approval, or stops at an embeddable component with no gating surface to verify.
clarifyingQuestions.assumptionIfUnanswered: This mission builds a minimal merge approval screen sufficient to embed the viewer and demonstrate that approval is blocked without a packet; the full merge-approval workflow remains a separate mission.

proposedLedgerEntry.statement: Deliver a v0 evidence viewer in Camino's GUI that renders evidence packets so merge approval happens against inspectable evidence (CAM-CORE-07, Phase-1 scope).

checklist.note: Top-level mission mandate; realized by the rendering component, artifact previews, and approval-screen integration.
Established repo-layout context; work lands in packages/gui (and packages/shared for types), but the layout itself is not an obligation of this mission.
Technology defaults; constrains the stack (TypeScript/Node 22, React+Vite, Playwright) used by every issue but is a standing default, not a new obligation.

proposedLedgerEntry.statement: v0 scope is functional rendering (packet contents + artifact previews); presentation polish is out of scope and follows later.

checklist.note: Scope boundary for the mission; bounds I2/I3 to functional rendering.
Context: no real packets exist, so the mission must supply synthetic fixtures to build and verify against (drives I5 and Q1).
Design rationale for prioritizing CAM-CORE-07; motivation, not an obligation.
Design rationale on (sha, base) identity and why only Camino-authored candidates are gating; informs I1/I2 typing but is context. The concrete obligation is captured in S12.
Motivation-section restatement that the v0 GUI includes an evidence viewer; the concrete obligation is S1/S9, so no separate ledger entry to avoid duplication.

proposedLedgerEntry.statement: The evidence viewer renders evidence packets (v1 schema) with artifact previews for logs, screenshots, and traces.
Every merge approval screen embeds the packet being approved with artifacts previewable and the gating/advisory distinction visible; no v1 merge is approvable without its packet.

checklist.note: Acceptance criteria for CAM-CORE-07; the gating-without-packet rule is verified in I4 and probed in I5.

proposedLedgerEntry.statement: Represent and render the full v1 evidence-packet schema (all per-attempt fields and nested command/artifact/check/review/exclusion/waiver lists).

checklist.note: Schema of record; typed in I1, rendered in I2.

proposedLedgerEntry.statement: Every packet item carries its own (sha, base_sha) identity and a class: advisory|gating marker, and the viewer surfaces that class per item.

checklist.note: Advisory/gating classification typed in I1 and shown per item in I2.
Mission-rollup schema (mission-level, registry-cited). Whether the v0 viewer renders it is underdetermined (see Q4); assumed deferred to a later mission, so no issue owns it here.

proposedLedgerEntry.statement: The viewer renders the advisory/gating distinction and the exclusion and waiver lists.

checklist.note: Explicit rendering obligations, realized in I2.
Escalation inbox (CAM-CORE-05) is a separate mission; listed for interface awareness only.
Mission→main PR lifecycle (CAM-MERGE-13) is a separate mission; the viewer's packets are what such PRs link to, but the PR flow is out of scope here.
